Container.Name: VideoWare
Container.Overview: Self-contained media upload and processing platform. Upload media, get fast previews (thumbnails, sprites), edit and render clips on a timeline, and run AI video analysis (object/face/person detection, tracking, shot changes, speech transcription) using FFmpeg and optional Google Cloud APIs. This single container bundles everything it needs: the Next.js web app, PocketBase database/API, the background worker (FFmpeg), and Redis (the worker's job broker) - no external services required. S3-compatible storage and Google Cloud are optional add-ons.
Container.Description:
VideoWare is a modern, self-hosted media platform built with Next.js, PocketBase, and a NestJS background worker.
This template runs the **monolithic** image, which bundles all services in one container: - **Next.js web app** - upload, browse, edit, and render media - **PocketBase** - database, realtime updates, auth, and REST API - **Background worker** - FFmpeg transcoding, thumbnail/sprite generation, clip rendering, and AI labels - **Redis** - internal BullMQ job broker (localhost only, not exposed) - **Nginx** - reverse proxy fronting everything on a single port
**Access after install:** - Web app: `http://[IP]:[PORT:80]/` - PocketBase admin: `http://[IP]:[PORT:80]/_/` - PocketBase API: `http://[IP]:[PORT:80]/api/`
**First run:** set a strong **Admin Password** - the PocketBase superuser is auto-created on startup from the Admin Email/Password you provide.
**Storage:** all persistent data (PocketBase DB, uploaded media, and the Redis queue) lives under the single `/data` mount, so the whole app is captured by one appdata path. Optional S3-compatible storage and Google Cloud Video Intelligence can be enabled via extra variables (see the project README).

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Host port for the web app, PocketBase API and admin (proxied by nginx on container port 80).

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: Persistent storage: PocketBase database, uploaded media, and the Redis queue.

Config.Name: Admin Email
Config.Description: Email for the auto-created PocketBase superuser.

Config.Name: Admin Password
Config.Description: Password for the auto-created PocketBase superuser. MUST be set to a strong value - leaving it at the default skips superuser creation.

Config.Name: Storage Type
Config.Description: Storage backend: 'local' (files under /data) or 's3'. For 's3' you must also supply the STORAGE_S3_* variables (see README).

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Logging verbosity: error, warn, info, debug, verbose.
